element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns directional control in air
Learn to position satellite independently from character
Strategic loadout selection before boss encounters
Adapt firepower to upcoming boss patterns
Resource management decision—when to spend vitality
Apply all core mechanics under pressure; learn attack patterns
Balance offensive spending (megacrush) vs. defensive needs

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension with strategic shop breaks. Enemy density escalates toward boss encounters. Multi-phase boss patterns create local tension cycles within each stage.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Zenny coin density and placement signal optional engagement areas; shops at fixed stage waypoints

difficulty_curve.early_game: Designed for accessibility: generous aiming decoupling, tutorial in mechanics via shop introduction, forgiving continue system. Development notes indicate Capcom reduced difficulty from earlier prototypes.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Enemy projectile patterns densify; boss complexity increases; resource management (Zenny/vitality) becomes tighter
notable_spikes: Boss fights at stage conclusions (pressure spikes relative to wave gameplay)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage follows: horizontal scrolling enemy gauntlet → item shop checkpoint with timed purchase window → boss encounter. Shop provides relief and strategic reset point.
Player 1 has long-range automatic rifle; Player 2 has short-range wide shot. Co-op encourages positioning specialization and mutual coverage.
Core mechanic: character moves in one direction while satellite/aim rotates freely in 16 directions. Decoupling reduces 'bullet hell' punishment vs. prior Capcom shooters.
